medications.name: Cefpodoxime
medications.instructions: Take 1 tablet by mouth every 12 hours

medications.name: Albuterol inhaler
medications.instructions: Inhale 2 puffs every 6 hours as needed
medications.notes: For wheezing or shortness of breath.

follow_up: Primary care visit with Dr. M. Shah within 7 days of discharge.
Repeat chest X-ray in 6 to 8 weeks to confirm clearing.
Call clinic if antibiotics cause rash, severe diarrhea, or vomiting.
return_precautions: Return to the emergency department for worsening shortness of breath, chest pain, confusion, lips turning blue, fever above 101.5 F after 48 hours of antibiotics, or oxygen saturation below 90% if measured at home.
risk_notes: Moderate risk: recent oxygen requirement and antibiotic completion needed.
